title: Director of Grounds
description: Lead and manage all grounds operations and maintenance for D.C. United's facilities, including stadiums and training grounds. Oversee grounds staff, develop maintenance protocols, and ensure facilities meet professional soccer standards. Responsible for field preparation, equipment management, and ensuring optimal playing surface conditions.

title: Data Scientist
description: The Data Scientist will support U.S. Soccer's technical and operational decisions through advanced analytics, data modeling, and insights generation. This role involves developing analytical frameworks, managing data infrastructure, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimize player development and organizational performance.

title: Team Administrator
description: Phoenix Rising FC is seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented individual to serve as Team Administrator responsible for day-to-day operations of the club. Key responsibilities include coordinating team travel, managing player contracts and registrations, overseeing team meals and catering, and supervising soccer operations interns. The role requires strong organizational skills, the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment, and willingness to work non-traditional hours.

title: Video Analyst, Academy
description: The Video Analyst is responsible for recording and analyzing video footage of all Academy training sessions and matches, collaborating closely with the Academy coaching and technical staff. The role includes filming and managing video recordings, preparing and sharing content with technical staff, analyzing footage, assisting with opponent scouting, and organizing training and game clips for Academy players. This position reports to the First Team Video Analyst and requires FAA Remote Pilot Certification, drone operation experience, and proficiency with video analysis tools such as Sportscode and HUDL.

title: Assistant Athletic Trainer
description: The Portland Fire are seeking a motivated Assistant Athletic Trainer to join their performance team and provide front-line medical care in support of player health and availability. The role involves assisting the Head Athletic Trainer with player training preparation, injury evaluation, acute care, rehabilitation directives, and sport science processes. This is an ideal opportunity for an early-career professional who is curious, adaptable, and eager to learn within a progressive, collaborative, and athlete-centered environment.

title: Head of Sports Science
description: Lead the sports science department at AFC Wimbledon, overseeing player performance analysis, injury prevention, and rehabilitation programs. Responsible for developing and implementing evidence-based sports science strategies to optimize player development and team performance across all age groups.

title: First Team Graduate Performance Coach
description: AFC Bournemouth is seeking a First Team Graduate Performance Coach to support the club's elite performance department. The role involves working with first-team players on physical conditioning, injury prevention, and performance optimization. The successful candidate will collaborate with medical, coaching, and analytics staff to enhance player development and team performance.

The role involves designing periodized strength and conditioning protocols, managing player recovery, and collaborating with medical and coaching staff to optimize athletic performance.

title: Academy Fitness Coach
description: Academy Fitness Coach responsible for developing and implementing fitness programs for youth players in Atlanta United's academy system. This role focuses on age-appropriate physical development and injury prevention for academy players.

title: Academy Fitness Coach
description: Atlanta United FC is seeking an Academy Fitness Coach responsible for on-field fitness, strength training, and load prescription and monitoring to progress players through the Development Pathway. This role blends Long-Term Athletic Development (LTAD) principles with evidence-based training methodologies to build resilient, well-rounded athletes.  Responsibilities: Physical Development & Training Delivery: Design and deliver age-appropriate on-field fitness sessions emphasizing movement quality, speed development, and aerobic capacity Lead strength training programs focused on fundamental movement patterns, injury resilience, and athletic foundations Integrate physical preparation into technical training environments in collaboration with coaching staff Conduct individualized training for players requiring additional physical development or returning from injury Educate players on the principles of training, recovery, and performance optimization Collaborate and support individual develop plan sessions Load Management & Monitoring: Collect and analyze GPS to inform training load decisions Monitor growth, maturation status (PHV), and readiness to inform individualized load prescription Communicate daily and weekly load summaries to coaching and medical staff Track training availability and flag concerning trends in workload tolerance or recovery Testing & Assessment: Administer standardized physical performance testing protocols (e.g., CMJ, speed gates, YoYo) Analyze and report testing results to inform individual development plans Collaborate with medical staff on pre-participation physical examinations and return-to-play assessments Player Development & Communication: Provide constructive feedback to players on physical performance and development trajectory Foster a growth mindset and resilience in young athletes navigating physical development Collaboration & Integration: Work closely with technical coaching staff to align physical preparation with tactical and technical periodization Partner with Academy medical staff on injury prevention strategies and return-to-play progressions Coordinate with the Head of Developmental Performance and Academy Fitness Coaches across all age groups to ensure continuity in the Development Pathway Contribute to Academy-wide performance data infrastructure and reporting standards Match Day & Travel: Attend home and away matches to oversee warm-ups, monitor substitutes, and manage in-game fitness interventions Support player recovery protocols on travel days and tournament weekends Coordinate with tournament hosts and facilities for appropriate training and recovery resources Required Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Exercise Science, Kinesiology, Sport Science, or related field CSCS (Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist) or equivalent certification Minimum 2 years of experience working with youth athletes in a team sport setting Demonstrated knowledge of Long-Term Athletic Development (LTAD) principles and youth training considerations Experience with GPS/wearable technology and athlete monitoring systems Strong understanding of periodization, load management, and fatigue monitoring Excellent communication skills with ability to work effectively with youth athletes, coaches, and parents Current CPR/AED and First Aid certification Willingness to work irregular hours including evenings, weekends, and travel Preferred Qualifications: Master's degree in related field CPSS (Certified Performance and Sport Scientist) certification Previous experience in soccer or academy football environment Experience with Catapult/STATSports/ PlayerData GPS, Vald ForceDecks, or similar technologies Bilingual (English/Spanish) preferred  We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, gender identity, marital or veteran status, or any other protected class.

title: Academy Dietician
description:
Position Overview
The Columbus Crew Academy Dietitian will provide nutrition services to Crew 2 and Academy athletes. Primary responsibilities include working as a part of a multi-disciplinary team, developing and streamlining nutrition education throughout the organization as a part of a One Club approach, facilitating the delivery of high-quality food, beverages and supplements to aid performance, providing 1:1 education to players and staff, and adhering to mission and values of the Columbus Crew organization.

Position Responsibilities
Implement Crew nutrition philosophies and standards across all age groups 
Oversee the fueling of our Crew 2 and Academy athletes
Provide performance nutrition education through created documents, presentations, digital communication and other various teaching methods to a variety of ages 
Conduct 1:1 nutrition consultations to provide individualized nutrition strategies for players based on needs, goals, and training phase
Assists with assessing dietary practices, body composition, hydration practices and energy balance (intake and expenditure) of athletes in the context of athletic performance and well-being.
Evaluates nutritional supplements, including herbal supplements, for legality, safety, quality, and efficacy, monitors the use of appropriate supplementation
Participate with athletic training, performance, and strength and conditioning staff to ensure a comprehensive approach for players rehabbing from an injury (Injury nutrition, return to play, pre- and post- surgery, etc.) 
Integrate with sport coaches, strength and conditioning staff, sports medicine staff to ensure a comprehensive approach to training, nutrition and recovery, as well as aiding in the treatment of nutritional- related medical issues
Ensure nutrition services at Columbus Crew are based on the latest, evidence-based practices in the field by staying up to date on scientific research and trends in performance nutrition
Coordinate meals with stadium locations, caterers and/or chefs at both home and road locations (snack bags, hotel menus, bus/airport meals, post-match meals, etc.) 
Occasional travel for matches and tournaments for Academy and Crew 2
Help to create a curriculum of culinary performance methods and techniques for Academy and Crew 2 (i.e. cooking classes/demos)
Maintain effective communication regarding athlete nutrition with the Head of Nutrition 
Attend various staff meetings as requested (medical, performance, return to play, etc.) 
Assist with off-season camps for Academy and Crew 2
Hours include nights, weekends, some holidays
Days may include long standing durations and lifting heavy objects 
Performs other duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in nutrition/dietetics or other related field from an accredited university or college
Completion of a coordinated and/or didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice program approved by the Commission on Accreditation for Dietetics Education (CADE) of the Academy of Nutrition & Dietetics
RD credential required (with active registration through CDR)
Minimum 2 years' experience working in athletics; specifically, youth and high school aged athletes
Obtain licensure in the state of Ohio prior to first day
Complete Safe Sport certifications prior to first day
Working towards obtaining CSSD (Certified Specialist in Sports Dietetics)
Must have an outstanding work ethic and attention to detail
Ability to maintain HIPPA confidentiality

Preferred Qualifications
Board certified specialist in sports dietetics (CSSD)
Master's degree in nutrition/dietetics or other related field from an accredited university or college
Previous experience in professional or collegiate soccer
synopsis: The Columbus Crew is seeking an Academy Dietician to provide nutritional guidance and support to academy players. This role involves developing and implementing nutrition programs to optimize player performance and health.

title: Head of Athletic Development
description:
Who We Are


Founded in 1882, Tottenham Hotspur is an iconic English football club, playing in the Premier League and Women’s Super League. From North London to the world, our fanbase spans continents, cultures, and generations. Spurs is a club that’s always dared to push boundaries, breaking new ground and rewriting history.


We offer world class facilities. In 2019, we opened our state-of-the-art Stadium, a £1 billion landmark that’s the beating heart of North Tottenham’s transformation. More than just a football ground, it’s an engine of change creating 4,000 jobs and injecting £300 million into the local economy every year.


We’re at our brightest when we’re all together. Our Club, our teams, our community.


There is only one Hotspur. Tottenham Hotspur.


Role Purpose & Candidate


Tottenham Hotspur Football Club is seeking an exceptional Head of Athletic Development to develop and lead the physical development strategy within the Men’s team in addition to providing technical support and development across Academy and Women’s teams. In the Men’s team, they will be responsible for the design and delivery of physical training and testing protocols, working in close partnership with the Coaching and Performance staff. In the Academy and Women’s programmes, they will provide strategic and technical support, ensuring a coherent, progressive, and philosophically aligned physical development pathway.


We are looking for a practitioner who combines deep technical expertise in athletic development with the leadership capability to drive standards across a complex performance organisation. The successful candidate will be both a high-calibre practitioner and a credible, influential leader with significant experience in delivering impact in high performance environments.


Safeguarding & Recruitment Statement


Safeguarding is fundamental to the success in all that we do. The Club follows a Safer Recruitment process for all roles. Successful candidates are to be reminded they would be subject to proportionate background, DBS and reference checks as required for this role.


We welcome applications from anyone regardless of age, disability, gender, race, or ethnic and national origins, religion or belief, or sexual orientation.
responsibilities:
Tottenham Hotspur Football Club is seeking an exceptional Head of Athletic Development to develop and lead the physical development strategy within the Men’s team in addition to providing technical support and development across Academy and Women’s teams. In the Men’s team, they will be responsible for the design and delivery of physical training and testing protocols, working in close partnership with the Coaching and Performance staff. In the Academy and Women’s programmes, they will provide strategic and technical support, ensuring a coherent, progressive, and philosophically aligned physical development pathway.


We are looking for a practitioner who combines deep technical expertise in athletic development with the leadership capability to drive standards across a complex performance organisation. The successful candidate will be both a high-calibre practitioner and a credible, influential leader with significant experience in delivering impact in high performance environments.

The successful candidate will be both a high-calibre practitioner and a credible, influential leader with significant experience in delivering impact in high performance environments.

title: Recruitment Data Analyst
description: The successful candidate will play a leading role in the identification , monitoring and recruitment of talent through the application of data analytics within the scouting and recruitment process. Reporting to the Head of Recruitment and working in close collaboration with the rest of the department, they will be responsible for the mana gement, analysis and integration of large data sets from various providers into the club’s bespoke recruitment system, having a significant impact within a professional football environment.
responsibilities: Interpret and manipulate data in accordance with the philosophy, current and long-term requirements of Cambridge United to highlight, monitor and analyse talent of interest from target leagues. • Present attractive and actionable statistical reports and visualisations giving insights to various personnel with a range of understanding. • Utilise all resources to deliver data-driven research projects. • Collaborate on the maintenance and development of the club’s bespoke recruitment system, integrating automated processes and optimising data delivery. • Discover and create new avenues to support and positively impact talent identification and monitoring.  This job description sets out the key outcomes required. It does not specify in detail the activities required to achieve these outcomes. As a term of your employment, you may reasonably be expected to perform duties of a similar or related nature to those outlined in the job description.
requirements_min:
Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science or related field
Proficiency in data analysis tools and software
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and presentation abilities
Passion for football and understanding of the game
requirements_pref:
Experience in sports analytics or football data analysis
Knowledge of statistical modeling and machine learning
Proficiency in R, Python, or similar programming languages
Experience with data visualization tools
Previous experience in professional sports environment
benefits: Annual Reviewed Industry Benchmarked Salaries • 21 Days Annual Leave + Bank Holidays (pro-rata, part-time) • One Day Per Week Remote Working • TOIL For Matchdays & Events worked • Mental Health Practitioners • Match Tickets • 10% Club Retail Discount • Club Partner Discounts
about_org: We believe that standing still in both football and business is not an option. We are on an exciting journey to reach the highest levels of English Football. Recent investment into training facilities and the purchase of the Cledara Abbey Stadium which will soon be redeveloped, optimises our aspiration to grow with one of the world’s fastest developing cities where the rules of Association Football were created. We pride ourselves on providing employees with an opportunity to succeed and value innovation, collaboration and personal growth. Most importantly we are dedicated to creating a supportive and inclusive environment, underpinned by our motto to be ‘United in Endeavour’.

title: Academy Nutritionist (p/t)
description: The Academy Nutritionist will play a pivotal role in supporting the holistic development of players across all phases of the Academy pathway. This role demands a proactive, detail-oriented practitioner who can integrate strategic monitoring, individualised performance support, and impactful education within a multidisciplinary environment. Working collaboratively with performance staff, players, parents, and host families, the successful candidate will ensure that nutrition provision consistently enhances health, wellbeing, and on-field performance while upholding the highest standards of safeguarding and governance.
responsibilities:
Strategic & Monitoring Responsibilities

· Contribute to the systematic collection of objective anthropometric data (height, weight, girths, and skinfolds) in accordance with club processes, ensuring timely feedback to relevant stakeholders, particularly within the PDP phase.
· Support the Individual Development Plan (IDP) process by collecting and interpreting player-specific objective data aligned with individual performance goals (PDP players).
· Collaborate with the Schoolboy Performance Team to ensure consistent monitoring of height and weight across U9–U16 age groups.
· Ensure all designated “priority” PDP players receive a minimum nutrition touchpoint every 4-weeks to review progress and update individual plans.
· Deliver individualised nutrition support to improve performance, health, and recovery.

Curriculum & Education Delivery

· Contribute to the ongoing review and evolution of the Academy nutrition curriculum, ensuring age-appropriate content across each stage of development.
· Deliver parent education workshops (TSG) tailored to key topics relevant to U9–U21 players.
· Provide educational sessions and workshops for U18 and Development Team players aligned with performance curriculum objectives.
· Develop and distribute educational materials to players and parents, including bespoke materials to support tours and competition periods.
· Provide guidance and ongoing education to host families, with regular monitoring of nutritional standards and provision.

Performance & Operational Integration

· Liaise with age-group performance staff and the performance chef to ensure optimal nutrition provision across all training and matchday scenarios.
· Collaborate with performance staff to implement group supplementation strategies and manage individual supplementation protocols within the PDP phase (informed by blood profiling where appropriate).
· Oversee and monitor daily fuelling, recovery, and operational processes to ensure consistent, high-quality service delivery with no delivery gaps.

Safeguarding & Governance

· Maintain and demonstrate exemplary safeguarding knowledge and practice, including full understanding of legislation, governing body regulations, and child protection policies.
· Ensure all safeguarding or welfare concerns are reported promptly and appropriately.
· Comply with all club policies and procedures, including health, safety, and security regulations.
requirements_min:
• Degree in Sports Science, Performance Analysis or related field
• Minimum 2 years experience in football performance analysis
• Proficiency in video analysis software (Hudl, Sportscode, etc.)
• Strong understanding of football tactics and technical aspects
• Excellent communication and presentation skills
• Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines
requirements_pref:
• Experience working in professional football environment
• Knowledge of statistical analysis tools
• UEFA coaching qualifications
• Experience with Premier League level analysis
about_org: Fulham Football Club is a professional football club based in London, England, competing in the Premier League. The club is committed to excellence both on and off the pitch, providing world-class facilities and opportunities for staff development.
synopsis: The Academy Nutritionist will play a pivotal role in supporting the holistic development of players across all phases of the Academy pathway. This role demands a proactive, detail-oriented practitioner who can integrate strategic monitoring, individualised performance support, and impactful education within a multidisciplinary environment. Working collaboratively with performance staff, players, parents, and host families, the successful candidate will ensure that nutrition provision consistently enhances health, wellbeing, and on-field performance while upholding the highest standards of safeguarding and governance.

title: Academy Data Scientist
description: This role will lead on turning complex football data into clear, actionable insights that support the Insights Department to make informed decisions across the Academy. Working across a broad range of data sources, this role will ensure Performance Analysts have access to reports and visualisations that enable effective team and individual analysis. In addition, this role will strengthen the Academy’s ability to monitor team and individual performance, as well as track player development through advanced statistical models.
responsibilities:
Develop interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI to enable real-time insights for decision making.
Develop and validate statistical and machine learning models that quantify player behaviours.
Ensure all modelling approaches are underpinned by robust, interpretable statistical principles.
Design and implement metrics that quantify player and team effectiveness.
Ensure efficient data storage, retrieval and processing of data.
Work closely with the Insights, Coaching, Recruitment and Performance Staff to utilise complex data to inform decision making across the Academy.
Build internal tools and visual applications to streamline internal processes and enhance workflow efficiency.
Communicate clearly to non-technical stakeholders, translating complex data outputs into football relevant insight.
Undertake continual professional development to ensure personal skills, knowledge and competence to fulfil the role.
Ensure all work is completed in a priority basis, meeting a high level of professionalism, quality, and craftmanship.
Stay up to date with the latest trends and best practice in data analytics to recommend new methodologies to enhance the Insights Department delivery.
Continually look to develop and improve the Insights Department data provisions.
Support the highest levels of safeguarding.
requirements_min:
A BSc in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Computer Science or Quantitative Related Field.
Strong proficiency in Power BI, Power Apps and Microsoft SQL Server.
Knowledge of football analytics, performance metrics and statistical models.
The ability to write code (R/Python) to create custom visuals.
Experience operating on and building from large datasets.
High attention to detail and be an innovate problem solver.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Strong presentation skills with the ability to create evidence-based case studies.
Curious nature to football problems and motivations for continuous learning.
requirements_pref:
A MSc in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Computer Science or Quantitative Related Field.
Completed courses or modules in Statistics or Data Science.
Experience using cloud-based analytics platforms (Snowflake or Databricks).
Experience working in a football environment.
synopsis: This role will lead on turning complex football data into clear, actionable insights that support the Insights Department to make informed decisions across the Academy. Working across a broad range of data sources, this role will ensure Performance Analysts have access to reports and visualisations that enable effective team and individual analysis. In addition, this role will strengthen the Academy’s ability to monitor team and individual performance, as well as track player development through advanced statistical models.

title: Player Services Officer
description:
We are looking for a dedicated and passionate individual to provide high-quality player services and operational support to our First Team players and senior management. By ensuring day-to-day needs are managed efficiently, you will help create an environment where players and staff can focus on delivering their best performance on and off the pitch.
 
Fluency in both English and French would be highly valued, as it enables us to better support our French-speaking players and contribute to an inclusive and supportive team environment.
responsibilities:
Player Services Support
Provide proactive day-to-day support to First Team players in line with the team schedule.
Support the onboarding and integration of new players, including relocation assistance for players and families moving to the UK or where English is not their first language.
Assist with transport arrangements for new players during their initial onboarding period.
Coordinate player and staff signing requests (e.g. shirts and photographs), maintaining accurate monthly records and reporting.
Manage incoming player mail and fan correspondence, ensuring appropriate responses are issued.
Provide additional support to French-speaking players and their families where required.
Operations & Administration
Provide general administrative and operational support across the First Team Operations and Player Services department.
Maintain accurate player and senior management records within internal systems.
Support personal administration for players and staff, including utilities and related documentation.
Coordinate vehicle logistics for players and staff, including lease handovers, servicing, MOTs, and management of driving or parking fines.
Coordinate internal post between Cobham Training Ground and Stamford Bridge.
Facilitate player and staff orders from the club shop.
Matchday & Training Ground Support
Provide operational and player services support on First Team matchdays, including assisting within the players’ family area.
Provide reception cover at Cobham Training Ground during weekend training sessions and at least one weekday per week.
requirements_min:
What You’ll Bring
     Strong organisational and administrative skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities.
     Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build positive relationships with players, staff, and families.
     High level of discretion and professionalism when handling confidential information.
     Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accurate records and documentation.
     Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.
     Proactive and solutions-focused, with the ability to anticipate needs and resolve issues quickly.
     Fluent in English; French language skills are highly valued to support French-speaking players and their families.
requirements_pref:
Our Expectations:

To embody the club’s BLUE behaviours (Brave, Lead, Unity, Edge) in the approach to work and interaction with others
To adhere to the club’s policies and procedures, including Health & Safety, Financial Authorisation, Confidentiality and GDPR.
To act as an ambassador for diversity, equality, and inclusion, and demonstrate a positive commitment by treating others fairly in line with our Equality, Diversity & Inclusion Policy and reporting any acts of discrimination through appropriate channels
To create a safe environment and act to protect all young people and vulnerable adults that are either in your care or attending club premises, and report any concerns to the Safeguarding Lead
To report any misconduct or suspected misconduct to the HR Department.
synopsis:
We are looking for a dedicated and passionate individual to provide high-quality player services and operational support to our First Team players and senior management. By ensuring day-to-day needs are managed efficiently, you will help create an environment where players and staff can focus on delivering their best performance on and off the pitch.
 
Fluency in both English and French would be highly valued, as it enables us to better support our French-speaking players and contribute to an inclusive and supportive team environment.

title: Sports Turf Technician
description:
Our Sports Turf team ensure all pitches and associated areas are prepared to elite performance standards. In this role you will be an active contributor to a high‑performance turf team, working closely with the Sports Turf Operations Manager and Head of Sports Turf to bring the pitch strategy to life. You will adapt to changing schedules, and drive innovation in pitch renovation.

 

This isn’t your typical 9–5 job, it’s a year-round commitment where you will experience all seasons, hot and cold!
responsibilities:
Are you enthusiastic, reliable and willing to learn? Do you take pride in your work and enjoy being outdoors in all seasons? If so, then we would love to hear from you.
• Deliver high quality sports turf maintenance to ensure all pitches and associated areas are prepared to elite performance standards.-quality sports turf maintenance to ensure all pitches and associated areas are prepared to elite performance standards.
• Be an active contributor to a high‑performance turf team, working closely with the Sports Turf 
Operations Manager and taking technical direction from the Head of Sports Turf to bring the pitch strategy to life.
• Collaborate daily with the Operations Manager and Senior Technicians to align priorities, adapt to changing schedules, and deliver seamless operational execution.
• Drive innovation in pitch renovation, supporting the Head of Sports Turf with projects to embed modern turf-care technologies and approaches.
• Own data‑driven maintenance practices by keeping precise, up‑to‑date digital or hard‑copy records that support compliance, operational insight, and continuous improvement.
• Champion high‑quality machinery use, operating equipment with confidence and care, and  ensuring tools are cleaned, stored, and maintained to extend lifespan and performance.
 Strengthen equipment reliability through thorough pre‑ and post‑use checks, escalating any issues quickly to the Workshop Manager or Workshop Assistant.
• Lead with a safety‑first mindset, embedding robust health and safety standards into every action, task, and environment.
• Work as part of a connected, cross‑functional Sports Turf team, to deliver consistent, process‑driven maintenance.
• Invest in your craft, attending relevant industry courses and staying curious about emerging turf management technologies, sustainable practices, and elite‑sport standards.
 
You don’t need formal qualifications to thrive in this role, we’re committed to supporting your development. You’ll have the opportunity to attend relevant training courses, including tractor driving, spraying, and operating ride-on mowers.

title: Data, Digital & AI Lead
description:
This is a unique and high impact opportunity to transform how the UK’s high-performance system uses data, digital tools and artificial intelligence.
As Data, Digital & AI Lead, you will lead the strategic delivery of the People and Performance pillars of UK Sport’s Data, Digital & AI Strategy  — shaping how athletes, coaches and practitioners learn, connect, collaborate and make decisions.
From building early AI driven prototypes, to developing systemwide digital capabilities, through to driving long -term planning for LA 2028 and Brisbane 2032 — your work will directly influence the future of UK high- performance sport.
This role is for someone who thrives in complexity, sees the possibilities of modern technology, and is energised by shaping a bold, insight driven future.
responsibilities:
Strategic Leadership
• Lead delivery of the People and Performance pillars of the UK Sport Data, Digital & AI Strategy
• Translate strategic ambitions into clear, practical implementation plans
• Shape system wide digital and data direction while supporting the Growth & Productivity pillars
Digital Delivery & Innovation
• Deliver early high value digital and AI solutions, including MVPs and proofs of concept
• Prioritise opportunities that can drive performance impact — including the LA28 cycle
• Guide build vs buy decisions, sequencing, and readiness
System & Stakeholder Engagement
• Work collaboratively with partners including UKSI, NGBs, Home Nations, BOA and BPA
• Drive adoption of system tools such as the Performance Hub and system membership models
• Support alignment with long- term system planning for Brisbane 2032
Partnerships & Supplier Management
• Develop strong, values aligned relationships with technology and delivery partners
• Oversee supplier ecosystems to ensure strong performance, value for money and ethical AI practice
Collaboration, Governance & Impact
• Embed best practice in digital governance, data ethics and trust across the system
• Demonstrate measurable system impact and ROI for digital and AI initiatives
• Contribute to building a culture of shared learning, insight and evidence based decisions
About you
You’re a strategic, forward thinking leader with a strong track record of delivering transformation in complex environments. You build relationships easily, influence effectively and move comfortably between high level system strategy and hand s on delivery.
requirements_min:
• Experience working in complex, multi stakeholder environments
• Strong strategic thinking — able to turn ideas into clear, actionable plans
• Proven ability to lead complex programmes and drive change at pace
• Excellent communication and influencing skills
• Confidence working with technical, commercial and delivery partners
• Experience leading or contributing to interdisciplinary teams
requirements_pref:
Leadership experience across digital, data or AI transformation
• Understanding of high-performance or elite performance environment s
• Experience designing or implementing digital/data operating models
• Knowledge of AI ethics, data governance or modern digital delivery practices
benefits: 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays, pension scheme, flexible working arrangements, professional development opportunities
about_org: UK Sport is the nation's high-performance sports agency, responsible for investing in Olympic and Paralympic sports to deliver medal success. We work with National Governing Bodies and athletes to provide world-class support services including sports science, medicine, technology and performance analysis to help British athletes compete and win on the world stage.
synopsis:
This is a unique and high impact opportunity to transform how the UK’s high-performance system uses data, digital tools and artificial intelligence.
As Data, Digital & AI Lead, you will lead the strategic delivery of the People and Performance pillars of UK Sport’s Data, Digital & AI Strategy  — shaping how athletes, coaches and practitioners learn, connect, collaborate and make decisions.
From building early AI driven prototypes, to developing systemwide digital capabilities, through to driving long -term planning for LA 2028 and Brisbane 2032 — your work will directly influence the future of UK high- performance sport.
This role is for someone who thrives in complexity, sees the possibilities of modern technology, and is energised by shaping a bold, insight driven future.

title: Physiotherapist - Men's First Team
description:
We are seeking a proactive and efficient professional with a calm and positive attitude and previous experience in delivering physiotherapy in an elite sports environment. You will need to be able to plan and deliver treatment and rehabilitation of sports injuries and have a significant breadth of treatment skills and methods commensurate with a diverse range of elite athletes. 

You will need to have clear, concise and accurate verbal and written communication skills and be able to thrive under pressure in a fast-paced environment. 

You must hold a full, UK driving licence and be able to work flexibility as this role will require you to work evenings, weekends and matchdays as and when required.

Provide physiotherapy assessment, treatment and rehabilitation for first team players
Work collaboratively with the medical team to develop and implement injury prevention strategies
Attend training sessions and matches to provide immediate care when required
Maintain accurate medical records and documentation
Liaise with external medical professionals when necessary
Support the physical preparation of players for training and competition

Degree in Physiotherapy and HCPC registration
Previous experience working in professional sport, preferably football
Strong knowledge of sports injuries and rehabilitation techniques
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment
Flexible approach to working hours including evenings and weekends

Experience working with elite athletes
Knowledge of strength and conditioning principles
Additional qualifications in manual therapy or dry needling
